index.title: Dalai Eej | Heritage Site & Resort at Lake Khövsgöl, Mongolia
index.description: Looking for places to stay in Khövsgöl Province? Discover our #1 best value lodge. Enjoy a scenic view sauna, complimentary breakfast, and Bradt Guide-featured hospitality.

gettingHere.title: How We Get to Lake Khövsgöl | Dalai Eej Resort
gettingHere.description: A curated guide on travelling from Ulaanbaatar to Lake Khövsgöl. Discover domestic flight details, scenic overland routes, and private transfer information.
gettingHere.openGraphTitle: The Journey to Lake Khövsgöl | Dalai Eej Resort
gettingHere.openGraphDescription: Whether you prefer a quick domestic flight to Murun or a scenic drive across the Mongolian steppe, discover the best ways to reach our shores.

almanac.title: The Almanac | A Guide to the Northern Frontier | Dalai Eej Resort
almanac.description: Field notes and historical archives on Lake Khövsgöl — from the steppe to the taiga, Mörön to Khatgal, and the ancient divide of the northern frontier.
almanac.openGraphDescription: A multi-chapter field guide to the northern frontier of Lake Khövsgöl — travel, history, and ecology before you reach our shores.
almanac.openGraphImageAlt: Lake Khövsgöl and the northern frontier at Dalai Eej Resort

murun.description: Mörön is a quirky northern hub—the Wes Anderson runway, Shükherch Gelenkhüü, and finding Dalai Eej on the road to the lake.
murun.ogImageAlt: Mörön Airport terminal, noted for its mid-century aesthetic

borders-and-industry.metaTitle: The Making of the North: Borders & Industry
borders-and-industry.description: From the Zasagt Khan Aimag to Khatgal's Qing border post and Mongolia's first Soviet wool factory—the geopolitical history of Khövsgöl.

forest-and-steppe.metaTitle: The Forest and the Steppe: An Ancient Divide
forest-and-steppe.description: Lake Khövsgöl sits on Asia's most enduring ecological border—the tension between the Buddhist steppe and the Shamanic taiga.
forest-and-steppe.ogImageAlt: The Uushigiin Övör Deer Stones at the ancient threshold of the steppe

khovsgol-and-baikal.metaTitle: Two Lakes, One Empire: Khövsgöl and Baikal
khovsgol-and-baikal.description: How the Treaty of Kyakhta severed Khövsgöl from its geological sister Lake Baikal—and why their 20th-century destinies diverged so dramatically.
khovsgol-and-baikal.ogImageAlt: The shores of Olkhon Island on Lake Baikal

fam.title: FAM Tour Application | Dalai Eej Resort
fam.description: Tour operators and travel agencies: apply for a complimentary familiarisation trip to experience Dalai Eej Resort firsthand.

hero.title: A Sanctuary on the Shoreline
hero.subtitle: Situated on the pristine shores of Lake Khövsgöl, Dalai Eej offers a refuge of comfort in the heart of the National Park.

items.yurts: Heritage Cabins & Yurts with Lake Views

dining.title: Flavours of the Steppe
dining.desc: Experience warm Mongolian hospitality. From hearty Khorkhog to fresh European favourites, our restaurant serves delicious meals prepared with local ingredients. Enjoy your morning coffee while watching the sunrise over the lake.

activities.title: Adventures on the Blue Pearl
activities.desc: The lake is your playground. Charter a motorboat to the islands, hike the surrounding larch forests, or experience the nomadic lifestyle on a guided horse trek through the valley.

relaxation.title: Disconnect to Reconnect
relaxation.desc: Far from the city lights and noise. Spend your evenings by a crackling bonfire, gazing at the Milky Way, or relaxing in our wood-fired sauna after a day of exploration.

testimonials.title: Guest Experiences
testimonials.subtitle: What our guests say
testimonials.heritage: Trusted by leaders, executives, and families since 1998.

review1.text: A peaceful, lovely stay on the quieter side of the lake. Family-run, with walking trails, a private sauna at the water's edge, and great food.
review1.author: Michael R.

review2.text: Maria was overwhelmed. Had a wonderful time with you and the family. Bless you.
review2.author: Werner & Maria

review3.text: The hospitality of the owner and staff was the most wonderful thing. I spent a quiet, peaceful time — a wonderful memory of Mongolia.
review3.author: Makoto

review4.text: Three unforgettable days with my partner. The setting beside the lake, the warmth of the owners, the quiet afternoons — easily the most memorable trip we've ever had.
review4.author: Billy P.

A quiet refuge on Lake Khövsgöl's eastern shore.
Wooden cabins, deep waters, slow days.
Northern Mongolia.
footer.address: Mergen's Ridge, eastern Lake Khövsgöl shore, Khövsgöl Province, Mongolia

almanacPage.title: A Guide to the Northern Frontier
almanacPage.hook: Lake Khövsgöl is not merely a destination; it is a geographic and cultural fault line. To travel to the Khaich Valley is to step into a region where the open Central Asian steppe abruptly ends and the dense Siberian taiga begins.
almanacPage.seoMeta: A multi-chapter field guide spanning travel logistics, provincial history, and ecology. Read in sequence as a comprehensive guide to the northern frontier, or jump to specific eras to deepen your sense of where you are going.

hero.title: How We Get to Khövsgöl
hero.subtitle: A comprehensive guide to travelling from Ulaanbaatar to the Khaich Valley.

toc.section1: I. The Quickest Route: Domestic Flights
toc.section2: II. The Overland Expedition: Driving the Steppe
toc.section4: IV. The Final Leg: Murun to the Resort

section1.title: The Quickest Route: Domestic Flights
section1.p1: The most efficient route to the resort begins with a domestic flight from Ulaanbaatar (UBN) to Murun Airport (MXV).
section1.p2: During the peak summer season, domestic flights run almost daily. The flight takes approximately one hour, with one-way tickets starting from 300,000 MNT per person.
section1.p3: Because July represents the absolute peak of the Mongolian summer, domestic flights sell out exceptionally quickly. We strongly advise securing your air travel as early as possible directly with the carriers:

miat.name: MIAT Mongolian Airlines
miat.desc: Check schedules and book directly on the

hunnu.name: Hunnu Air
hunnu.desc: View timetables and book directly on the

chinggis.name: Chinggis Airlines (Nomin Holdings)
chinggis.desc: For availability on this newer premium option, please contact their operator, Tumee, directly via Viber at +976 77 809010.

section2.title: The Overland Expedition: Driving the Steppe
section2.p1: For those who wish to experience the vastness of the Mongolian landscape at their own pace, driving overland is a spectacular option. The 1,000 km route from Ulaanbaatar is fully paved.
section2.p2: If you plan to complete the drive to Lake Khövsgöl in a single day, an early departure is essential. We strongly advise setting out from Ulaanbaatar no later than 9:00 AM sharp. To comfortably complete the journey and arrive before nightfall, aim to safely maintain a cruising speed of 100 km/h where the open road allows.
section2.p3: We highly recommend breaking up the trip with a stop in Erdenet for lunch and refueling. As one of Mongolia's wealthiest cities per capita, Erdenet offers a charming glimpse into Soviet-era urban planning. Filled with character, you will find an eclectic mix of options to recharge, from cosy local "tea houses" to excellent standalone restaurants.
section2.p4: As you leave Erdenet and continue northwest, the road opens up into Bulgan province. If you are familiar with airag—Mongolia's traditional fermented mare's milk—Bulgan is the undisputed heartland for it. As you drive through, the definitive place to stop and taste the best airag in the country is at the Tuluu Pass (Түлүүгийн даваа).

section3.title: Intercity Coaches & Railway Options
section3.p1: There is no direct train to Murun; the railway ends in Erdenet. However, the route is a spur of the legendary Trans-Siberian Railway—the very same iconic railway famously travelled by David Bowie. Taking the overnight sleeper train to Erdenet is a deeply nostalgic, romantic way to travel. Once you arrive, independent drivers are waiting at the station ready to take you the rest of the way directly to Khatgal and the lake (please remember to negotiate your fare to your final destination before departing). Tickets available via the
section3.vipCoach: Departs once a week on Sundays at 21:10. This premium option features spacious, reclining seating and phone charging ports so you can sleep comfortably and wake up in the north.
section3.standardCoach: Depart six times a day, offering flexibility for your schedule.
section3.bookingCoach: Tickets can be purchased online via the Transdep E-Ticket Portal.
section3.p2: For a direct overland route, air-conditioned intercity coaches depart from Ulaanbaatar's Old Dragon Terminal (Хуучин Драгон). Tickets are 110,000 MNT for adults and 55,800 MNT for children (ages 6–12). All tickets automatically include travel insurance (up to 10 million MNT in compensation).
section3.motorbike: You can transport your motorbike on the train to Erdenet, bypassing the heaviest city traffic and officially beginning your ride from there.
section3.bowieCaption: David Bowie on the Trans-Siberian Railway, 1973.
section3.bowieAlt: Archival photograph of a Trans-Siberian Railway sleeper compartment.

section4.title: The Final Leg: Murun to the Resort
section4.p1: If you are driving yourself, Murun is your final major outpost. Before you leave the city limits for the lake, we highly recommend a stop at the Nomin Supermarket. Depending on your planned level of adventure, this is the definitive place to load up on essential supplies—from rechargeable batteries and flashlights to barbecue provisions and rain boots.
section4.p2: The resort is located 13 km beyond the village of Khatgal, tucked into the Khaich Valley on the eastern shore of the lake. If you are driving this final leg, please note that while our Google Maps pin is highly accurate, the final approach to the resort can be adventurous.
section4.p3: The first 8 km out of Khatgal is a graded gravel road, but the final 5 km is an unpaved, natural dirt track. Because of this, we require travelling in a 4x4 vehicle and highly recommend having an experienced driver at the wheel.
section4.note: Once you pass the Khatgal village edge, you will need to navigate around the tree line.
section4.p4: If you did not drive your own vehicle to Murun, you have two options for the final stretch:

transfer1.title: Resort Transfer
transfer1.body: We offer a private transfer—a 100 km drive followed by a scenic speedboat arrival. Share your itinerary with us to time your welcome perfectly.

transfer2.title: UBCab
transfer2.body: You can reliably hail a taxi directly from Murun to Khatgal using the UBCab app.
